title: Athyrium thelypteroides
taxonomy: Athyrium thelypteroides is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. It belongs to the genus Athyrium, which comprises several species of ferns. The genus Athyrium is a part of the order Polypodiales, which includes a wide variety of ferns. The class Polypodiopsida is the larger group that includes the order Polypodiales, along with several other orders of ferns. This plant's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other fern species.

intro: Athyrium thelypteroides, also known as the autumn fern or Japanese lady fern, is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae, native to East Asia. The health benefits of Athyrium thelypteroides include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Athyrium thelypteroides is often processed into herbal preparations such as tea, powder, and decoctions, which are used to promote digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues.
